title: podman machine and WSL desynchronized
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->
I was testing podman-desktop so it proposed me to upgrade from 4.0.2 to 4.1.1 which I did. But not it seems the machine state and WSL are desynchronized as I can send podman commands but the machine state seems to be done.
```

C:\Users\JeffMAURY\.crc\bin>podman machine list --format json
[
    {
        "Name": "podman-machine-default",
        "Default": true,
        "Created": "2022-07-18T14:29:18.2670252+02:00",
        "Running": false,
        "Starting": false,
        "LastUp": "2022-07-29T10:13:44.990707+02:00",
        "Stream": "35",
        "VMType": "wsl",
        "CPUs": 0,
        "Memory": "0",
        "DiskSize": "3186622464",
        "Port": 56786,
        "RemoteUsername": "user",
        "IdentityPath": "C:\\Users\\JeffMAURY\\.ssh\\podman-machine-default"
    }
]
```
```

**Describe the results you received:**

**Describe the results you expected:**

States should be in sync

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body: If you stop and start the machine on the PowerShell/ Windows Terminal CLI (podman machine stop, podman machine start) does the state recover, or does it still indicate its down when it is not?
I can't because if I stop it podman says it's not started and if I start it it fails (probably because the WSL is running)
ah ok in that case do a `wsl --shutdown` then try podman machine start/stop
OK now it's back to normal state. The question is what leads to that state ? Will try to reboot as I asked W11 to restore my applications when login

Not sure yet how it got into this state. There is a case where older 4.0.x machines are upgraded since the state format added fields. It could be that the machine was running when the windows installer ran and updated podman, and perhaps stop isnt on that transition path, so then you are stuck with no transition to go to. 

You probably will also have a stale win-sshproxy.exe hanging around after this. To kill those (after stopping) you can do `taskkill /f /im win-sshproxy.exe`
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
I wasn’t able to reproduce this one with current releases, so I suspect this issue is no longer present, feel free to reopen if it pops up again.
